site_context.name: CP Nelson (Hall Mines Smelter)
site_context.contamination_profile: Historic copper/lead smelter on Kootenay Lake -- extreme metals contamination (Cu, Zn, Pb, Cd, As), no organic contaminant pathway, no AVS/SEM data available.
site_context.narrative: CP Nelson (Site 0311) is a former Hall Mines copper smelter site on a freshwater lake in the Kootenays. It has 8 stations (5 Tier 1 co-located + 2 Tier 2b + 1 reference), with extreme metal contamination but no AVS/SEM data and no organic pathway data. The site-specific model produces a null result in screening mode (all predictions near-uniform) but corrects to 8/8 in assessment mode when biological effects are entered as hard evidence. This demonstrates the value of dual-mode inference.

coverage_table.data_type: 7 Metals (Cu, Zn, Pb, Cd, Hg, As, Cr)

coverage_table.note: AVS/SEM not measured at this site. metal_bioavail is NULL for all stations. This is the primary data gap driving the screening-mode null result.
7 stations with amphipod data (SED19-REF2 has tox only, no taxa).
5 co-located T1 stations with benthic community data.
Diversity not reported for this site.
Not applicable -- smelter site with no organic contaminant pathway.

key_gaps: No AVS/SEM data -- metal_bioavail is NULL for all 8 stations. This is the critical gap: without bioavailability modifiers, the screening model cannot resolve the metals-to-effects chain, producing near-uniform posteriors.
No organic pathway data (PAHs/PCBs) -- same as Toquaht, marginalization applies.

fitting_approach.summary: Hierarchical borrowing from the general model with organic pathway marginalization. The missing AVS/SEM data means the metal_bioavail node is NULL at all stations, causing the screening model to produce near-uniform posteriors. Assessment mode bypasses this gap by injecting observed biological effects as hard evidence.
fitting_approach.marginalization_scope: organic_bioavail parent of tox_amphipod and taxa_richness (same pattern as Toquaht)

site_scoped_overrides.rationale: organic_bioavail is NULL at all CP Nelson stations (no PAH/PCB data). Without marginalization, amphipod observations would be discarded from CPT fitting.
Same as tox_amphipod: organic_bioavail is NULL at all stations.

cpt_tiers.data_status: Parent space too large for 8 stations.

cpt_tiers.method: BDeu + general model priors, refined by site data
cpt_tiers.data_status: metal_bioavail effectively unlearnable (all NULL). organic_bioavail marginalized.

cpt_tiers.method: BDeu + general model priors with marginalization override
cpt_tiers.data_status: 7 amphipod observations, 5 taxa observations recovered via marginalization.

residual_outliers.explanation: In screening mode, the model produces a near-uniform posterior (0.35/0.34/0.32) because the missing AVS/SEM data prevents the metal pathway from propagating to effects. The Low prediction is essentially a coin flip. In assessment mode with effects entered as evidence, the model correctly predicts High (P=0.92).

Near-uniform screening posterior (0.32/0.33/0.35). The High prediction is not meaningful -- the model lacks the bioavailability data needed to discriminate. Assessment mode correctly predicts Moderate (P=0.43).
Similar to SED19-4: near-uniform screening posterior driven by missing AVS/SEM. Assessment mode correctly predicts Moderate (P=0.43).
Reference station with moderate observed risk. Screening posterior is near-uniform (0.31/0.33/0.36). Assessment mode correctly predicts Moderate (P=0.43).

governance.scope_note: Site-scoped organic pathway marginalization (same pattern as Toquaht). The screening-mode null result is a known consequence of missing AVS/SEM data, not a model defect. Assessment mode demonstrates the model works correctly when biological effects are available. AVS data recovery at CP Nelson remains the highest-leverage improvement action.

hitl_decisions.decision: Accepted CP Nelson screening-mode null result as a known limitation of missing AVS/SEM data. Assessment mode (8/8 correct) demonstrates model validity when effects are available.

applicability_notes: Screening mode is unreliable at this site due to missing AVS/SEM -- use assessment mode when biological effects are available.
AVS data recovery at CP Nelson is the highest-leverage action for improving screening-mode performance.
The null result in screening mode is consistent with the general model's behavior at sites without bioavailability modifiers.

hitl_review_questions.question: Is the screening-mode null result (1/8 correct, near-uniform posteriors) an acceptable known limitation?
hitl_review_questions.context: All 8 stations lack AVS/SEM data, which means metal_bioavail is NULL. Without bioavailability modifiers, the screening model cannot resolve the metals-to-effects causal chain. Assessment mode corrects all 8 predictions when biological effects are entered as hard evidence.

hitl_review_questions.question: Should AVS/SEM data recovery be prioritized for CP Nelson?
hitl_review_questions.context: All 8 high-toxicity stations at CP Nelson lack AVS data. Recovery of this data would allow the screening model to resolve metal bioavailability and produce meaningful predictions without requiring observed biological effects.

hitl_review_questions.question: Is the assessment mode performance (8/8 correct) sufficient to validate the site pack?
hitl_review_questions.context: Assessment mode uses observed biological effects as hard evidence (P=1.0), which bypasses the AVS gap. This is not circular -- it tests whether the model correctly integrates effects with chemistry to produce the right risk classification.

hitl_review_questions.question: Should this site pack be promoted beyond prototype stage given the screening-mode limitations?
hitl_review_questions.context: The pack demonstrates the value of dual-mode inference but has no practical screening-mode capability. Promotion may be premature until AVS data is recovered or the model is enhanced with alternative bioavailability proxies.
